questions: Is knee replacement surgery covered in the first year of the policy?

answers.answer: Knee replacement surgery is not covered in the first year of the policy due to the 24-month waiting period for joint replacement surgery.
answers.justification: The policy excludes expenses related to the treatment of listed conditions, including joint replacement surgery, until the expiry of 24 months of continuous coverage.

relevant_clauses.content: Expenses related to the treatment of the listed Conditions, surgeries/treatments shall be excluded until the expiry of 24 months of continuous coverage after the date of inception of the first Global Health Care Policy with Us.
